whiteness being present, not[ the hair would be
white ], not[ the hair would be black ]

what is neither bad nor good is not yet bad, and
others in which this has already become bad

g.title: tlg0093_tlg004.P151115
g.text: Place a piece of lead over vinegar in closed jars,
.Wait for lead to acquire thickness .Open the
jars, .Scrape away from the lead the substance
that has formed on its surface, repeating this
until you have consumed it completely, .Grind what
you have scraped away, .Filter off what you are
grinding, .The final precipitate is lead white

g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.T213927
g.text: In order for (white and effective) lead white to

g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.P213927
g.text: {1st Dioscorides recipe} To produce white and
effective lead white in the best variety, as well
as in worse varieties: .Pour most pungent vinegar
into a jar, .Place a piece of lead on a lattice,
.Lay the lattice with the lead onto the mouth of
the jar, .Above, throw over covers, .Wait until
the lead, crumbling down, has strained through the
lattice, .Decant away the clean fluid that floats
on top of the vinegar, .Pour the remaining viscous
fluid into a vessel, .Let the viscous fluid dry
under the sun, .Grind the dried substance, .Sift
the ground substance, .The substance that is
sifted away is the better variety of white and
effective lead white, .Grind and sift what remains
above the sieve of the solid substance {, repeat,
and do it again a third and fourth time}, .The
substances that are sifted away at each repeated
grinding and sifting are progressively worse
varieties of white and effective lead white

g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.T175521
g.text: The better variety, first to be sifted away, of
(white and effective) lead white should be picked

g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.T165902
g.text: (In order for (white and effective) lead white to

ophthalmological properties - as well as in worse
pungent vinegar into a vessel {as per phase 1 of
the 1st recipe for lead white} ), (. They place a

2 of the 1st recipe for lead white} ), .They lay a
piece of wood in the middle of the vessel, .They
place the lattice (with the leaden brick) on top
of the piece of wood in such a way that the former
does not touch the vinegar, .They close the mouth
of the vessel (with a lid), .They smear it all
around (in such a way as to seal it), .They leave
the thus closed vessel alone (under the sun) {for
10 days}, .Removing the lid, they look into the
they do the same as in {phases 6 to 13 of the 1st

content dry), (. One does the same as in phases 9
